key_departures.impact: Phillips was acquired at the 2025 trade deadline for a 3rd-round pick and immediately became the Eagles' top pass rusher, posting 2 sacks and 34 QB pressures in 8 games. His departure leaves a gaping hole at the top of the EDGE depth chart. The Eagles missed on Trey Hendrickson (Ravens, 4yr/$112M) and are currently pursuing a Jonathan Greenard trade but nothing is confirmed.

Blankenship started 50 games across four seasons as an undrafted free agent success story and was a team captain in 2025. His departure leaves a significant void at starting safety. Andrew Mukuba suffered a season-ending injury in 2025, leaving the Eagles extremely thin at the position. Michael Carter II (renegotiated) can play some safety snaps but is not a starter-caliber solution.

Dean posted a career-high 128 tackles in 2024 and had 55 tackles and 4 sacks in 10 games in 2025 before injury. His departure is somewhat mitigated by the presence of 2025 first-round pick Jihaad Campbell — however, Campbell is dealing with a shoulder injury that will keep him out most of the offseason. The LB depth behind Campbell is thin.

Dotson was acquired in a 2024 trade from Washington and totaled 37 catches for 478 yards and 1 TD across two Eagles seasons. He was a depth/rotational receiver, not a true starter. His departure thins a WR room that is already dealing with the ongoing A.J. Brown trade uncertainty.

Jackson started 10 games for the Eagles in 2025 as the CB2 opposite Quinyon Mitchell, posting 11 passes defended and 1 interception. He is an unrestricted free agent and has not signed elsewhere as of this writing. His role is functionally being replaced by the signing of Riq Woolen. Departure does not escalate need since Woolen was signed expressly as his replacement.
Reserve guard/tackle who started 1 game in 2025. Depth-only departure; does not create a meaningful need.
Versatile OL backup who made 4 starts in 2025 and could play center. His departure is a mild depth loss but does not escalate a core need.
Uche had 1 sack and 15 QB pressures in 12 games as a rotational edge rusher. His departure further depletes an already thin EDGE room post-Phillips.
Depth edge rusher who did not contribute significantly. Minor loss but adds to the cumulative EDGE attrition.
Backup QB who served as emergency QB2 behind Tanner McKee. Minor departure; QB depth need remains but was never a critical concern with McKee under contract.

key_departures.new_team: TBD — void date extended to March 13, negotiations ongoing
key_departures.impact: Goedert had a career year in 2025: 60 catches, 591 yards, 11 TDs (Eagles franchise record for TDs by a TE). His contract void date was pushed back from March 11 to give both sides time to negotiate a new deal. As of March 13, no new contract has been announced. If he departs, TE becomes a critical need. The Eagles have signed Mundt and re-signed Calcaterra as depth, but neither is a starting-caliber starter. FLAGGED AS PENDING.

signings.analysis: Three-year, $78M extension with $65M guaranteed makes Davis the highest-paid nose tackle in NFL history. He had a breakout 2025 season: 4.5 sacks, 72 tackles, 9 TFL. The extension — signed before free agency even opened — locks the interior DL anchor through 2029. IDL is no longer a need; the pairing of Davis and Jalen Carter (on his rookie deal, 5th-year option decision pending by May 1) gives Philadelphia one of the best IDL duos in football. This position can drop multiple tiers.

One-year deal worth up to $15M. Woolen replaces Adoree' Jackson as the CB2 opposite Quinyon Mitchell, with Cooper DeJean sliding to full-time nickel. He is a Pro Bowl-caliber athlete (6-4, 4.26 40, 6 INTs as a rookie) who posted the lowest completion rate in man coverage of any defender in 2025 (27.6%). However, the 1-year structure is explicitly a prove-it deal after he lost his starting job in Seattle. This is a high-upside gamble: if it works, the CB room is elite; if it doesn't, the Eagles face a CB need again in 2027. Need is partially addressed — the starting role is filled for 2026, but no long-term commitment means CB remains a position to watch in the draft for future depth/succession planning.

One-year deal for a veteran blocking TE. The Eagles needed a dedicated blocking tight end after lacking one in 2025, which hampered the run game. Mundt (9 catches, 111 yards in 2025 with Jacksonville) addresses that specific blocking role. However, this move does nothing to replace Dallas Goedert as a pass-catching starter — the TE need at the top of the depth chart is unaddressed (and Goedert's status is still pending). Minimal need impact.

One-year re-sign for Calcaterra, a pass-catching backup TE with 42 career catches. He has been Goedert's primary backup and provides reliable depth. Combined with Mundt, the Eagles have functional TE2/TE3 pieces in place, but if Goedert departs, neither is a TE1. Minimal need impact.

Four-year, $14M re-sign with $7M guaranteed locks down one of the NFL's best punters (49.9 yards per punt in 2025, 6th in league; 82.3 PFF grade). This was a potential need — Mann was a UFA — but the Eagles moved quickly to retain him. Punter is fully addressed through 2029.

Carter II had his contract renegotiated downward from a $10.1M+ cap hit to avoid being cut, freeing Eagles cap space while retaining a versatile DB who can play nickel corner and safety. He will compete for time but is not a starter-caliber safety. With Blankenship gone, this is a temporary plug. S need remains.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Jaelan Phillips (the team's best pass rusher) signed a 4yr/$120M deal with Carolina. Uche (rotational) went to Miami. Ojulari (depth) went to Atlanta. The remaining EDGE corps is Nolan Smith Jr., Jalyx Hunt, and Jose Ramirez — none of whom have established themselves as impact starters. The Eagles missed on Hendrickson (Ravens) and are pursuing a Greenard trade (unconfirmed as of March 13). EDGE is now the team's most critical need. Upgrade from tier 2 to tier 1 critical.

Reed Blankenship (starter, team captain) signed a 3yr/$24.75M deal with Houston. Andrew Mukuba suffered a season-ending injury in 2025. The only remaining safety options are Michael Carter II (renegotiated, can play some safety snaps) and depth. The Eagles have no starting-caliber safety under contract. This is a co-critical need alongside EDGE. Upgrade from tier 3 to tier 1.

Dallas Goedert (11 TDs, 60 catches in 2025) is in active negotiations as of March 13 with his void date extended. If he departs, the Eagles would have only Calcaterra, Mundt, Cameron Latu, and E.J. Jenkins at TE — none are remotely capable of replacing Goedert's production. Should Goedert sign elsewhere, TE immediately becomes a tier 1 critical need. Flag as PENDING — hold at tier 1 until Goedert's situation resolves. If Goedert re-signs on a new deal, TE drops back to tier 4+.

Nakobe Dean (3yr/$36M, Raiders) departed. Jihaad Campbell (2025 first-round pick) is the assumed heir but is out most of the offseason with a shoulder injury (dealt with it all of 2025 despite pre-draft surgery). Campbell's health and timeline create real uncertainty. Zack Baun is under contract as depth. The Eagles need a LB2 opposite Campbell. Upgrade from tier 3 to tier 2.

Jordan Davis locked up on a 3yr/$78M deal with $65M guaranteed (highest-paid nose tackle in NFL history). Jalen Carter remains on his rookie deal (5th-year option decision pending by May 1; extension likely). The IDL pairing of Davis and Carter is elite. This is no longer a need. Drop from tier 2 to tier 4.

Adoree' Jackson (CB2 starter) departed. Riq Woolen signed a 1-year deal (up to $15M) as the replacement. Quinyon Mitchell and Cooper DeJean are two of the best young CBs in the NFL. Woolen's talent is undeniable but his 1-year deal is a prove-it structure — his inconsistency (lost his starting job in Seattle) means CB remains a watchable position in the draft for long-term depth and eventual succession. Drop from tier 2 to tier 3.

Jahan Dotson (rotational) departed to Atlanta. No WR signings made. A.J. Brown trade situation remains unresolved and is a wildcard that could escalate WR to tier 1 instantly if he is moved. With Brown retained, DeVonta Smith and Brown form one of the NFL's best duos and WR is not a pressing need. However, without a true WR3 replacement for Dotson and the A.J. Brown situation looming, WR stays at tier 2.

key_additions.headline: Highest-paid nose tackle in NFL history locked up through 2029 after breakout 4.5-sack, 72-tackle 2025 season
Super Bowl champion and Pro Bowl corner joins elite Philadelphia secondary to replace Adoree' Jackson as CB2
Best punter in franchise history locked up through 2029 on new multi-year deal
Veteran blocking TE addresses Eagles' run-game deficiency in tight end room
Pass-catching backup TE returns for fifth season to provide depth behind Dallas Goedert situation

key_losses.headline: Eagles' top pass rusher departs on elite deal after trade deadline acquisition; leaves critical EDGE void
Undrafted free agent success story and team captain departs, leaving Eagles without a starting safety
Starting linebacker departs on big deal; Eagles rely on injured Jihaad Campbell to fill void in 2026

key_losses.contract_summary: PENDING — void date extended to March 13, 2026
key_losses.headline: Eagles TE record-holder (11 TDs in 2025) in contract limbo; departure would create immediate critical need at TE
Rotational receiver departs, further thinning a WR depth chart already under A.J. Brown trade uncertainty

pending_situations.status: Void date on existing contract extended to March 13, 2026. Eagles and Goedert in active negotiations for a new deal. If no deal is reached, Goedert becomes a free agent. Any new deal expected to be a 1-year structure given his age (31) and Eagles' cap constraints. Resolution will dramatically alter TE need tier.
pending_situations.if_retained: TE drops to tier 4 — fully addressed for 2026.
pending_situations.if_departed: TE escalates to tier 1 — most critical need on roster alongside EDGE and S.

pending_situations.status: NOT CONFIRMED. Eagles have inquired about a trade and spoken to Greenard's agent about a potential extension. No deal reached as of March 13, 2026. If completed, would likely require a Day 2 draft pick and new contract. Would partially address EDGE need.
pending_situations.if_acquired: EDGE drops from tier 1 to tier 2 — partially addressed with a proven starter.
pending_situations.if_not_acquired: EDGE remains tier 1 — must be addressed in draft (early round) or via another FA/trade.

pending_situations.status: Eagles must decide on Carter's 5th-year option ($27.13M) by May 1. Extension talks expected. If extended, IDL need is locked up long-term. If option declined and Carter departs next offseason, IDL resurfaces as a need in 2027.
pending_situations.if_departed_next_offseason: IDL resurfaces as tier 2+ need in 2027 draft.

notes: All contract data sourced from NFL.com, ESPN, OverTheCap, Spotrac, and beat reporters (Inquirer, NBC Sports Philadelphia, PhillyVoice). Riq Woolen guaranteed money not yet reported — total value is 'up to $15M' per NFL Network/ESPN. Mundt and Calcaterra contract financial terms not publicly reported. Goedert's situation is the single largest variable in this assessment and will require a same-day update when resolved. Jonathan Greenard trade is inquiry/rumor only — not included in signings. Eagles' post-FA cap space of ~$11.52M per OverTheCap as of March 13.
